input.background: In 1978–79 a random sample of 1007 residents of the Lothian region were surveyed about their alcohol consumption in the previous seven days. In 1981, a follow-up survey was conducted among 676 'regular drinkers' from the original sample to study changes in alcohol consumption after an increase in alcohol prices. The follow-up survey reported a reduction in alcohol consumption and adverse effects, attributing the changes to the rise in alcohol prices.
input.question: Comment on the authors' interpretation of the results. Would your opinion be different if they had interviewed all 1007 subjects in the second survey?

output.answer: The interpretation is not valid for the reasons noted above.
